pages.text: $(bold)Train Schedule$() — a programmable itinerary: go to this station, wait for this condition, then move on. Conditions can be timers, cargo levels, redstone, or idle time. This is how trains run themselves.$(br2)You write a schedule on the item, then hand it to the train's controls.
pages.title: Schedules

pages.text: $(bold)Train Signals$() keep multiple trains from colliding. They divide track into $(italic)sections$() and hold a train at a red signal until the section ahead is clear — exactly like real block signalling.$(br2)$(li)$(bold)Entry/Chain signals$() — manage junctions and queues.$(li)$(bold)Observers & Display Links$() — report train arrivals to boards.
pages.title: Signals
